title: The Second Sunday in Lent
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
March 18, 2019 | Preacher: Katie Pearson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Second Sunday in Lent, falling within the traditional six-week Lenten season leading to Easter (April 21, 2019). Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would have included Genesis 12:1-4a (God's call to Abram), Psalm 121, Romans 4:1-5, 6-12, and John 3:1-17 (the Nicodemus passage emphasizing being born again). This Sunday emphasizes Abram's faith and trust in God's promises, central themes during mid-Lent spiritual discipline.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was navigating post-2018 General Convention decisions, including the canonically significant changes to marriage canon language approved in July 2018 that officially opened marriage to same-sex couples. The Diocese of Colorado, covering the state, was in the tenure of Bishop Rob Maloney (serving since 2015). No major diocesan events were scheduled immediately around mid-March, though the spring season typically brought increased confirmation and baptism schedules.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing early spring weather patterns with temperatures in the 40s-50s Fahrenheit and remnants of winter snow in the foothills. The Colorado General Assembly was actively in session (through May 8, 2019), with legislative attention on education funding and healthcare policy. The downtown Denver area, where the Cathedral is located, was in the midst of ongoing downtown development projects and discussions about homelessness and housing affordability that had become central civic concerns.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Mueller Investigation into 2016 election interference was approaching its conclusion, with Special Counsel Robert Mueller expected to deliver his report within days (released March 22, 2019). The 2020 presidential campaign was intensifying with Democratic candidates announcing or preparing announcements. The Boeing 737 MAX was grounded globally on March 13, 2019, following two fatal crashes, creating significant news about aviation safety and corporate accountability.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The New Zealand Christchurch mosque shootings (March 15, 2019) were the immediately preceding international crisis, killing 50 people and shocking the world with a white supremacist terrorist attack. Brexit negotiations continued with the United Kingdom facing the March 29 deadline, creating uncertainty and political turmoil in Britain. Venezuela remained in humanitarian crisis under Nicolás Maduro's regime with ongoing displacement and international diplomatic tension.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture was in a state of political polarization and anxiety, with the Mueller Report countdown creating intense partisan speculation. The cultural zeitgeist reflected concerns about gun violence in the wake of the Christchurch shooting and renewed conversations about extremism and online radicalization. In Denver specifically, the city maintained its reputation as a progressive, growth-oriented metropolitan area with strong arts and outdoor recreation culture; March Madness college basketball was in full cultural swing nationwide.
